theory.intro: Multiplying or dividing a decimal by $10$ or $100$ moves every digit to a new place-value column.
facts: $\times 10$ makes each digit $10$ times bigger → digits shift **one place left**.
$\div 10$ (or $\tfrac{1}{10}$ of) makes each digit $10$ times smaller → digits shift **one place right**.
steps: Decide the direction: bigger (×) shifts left, smaller (÷) shifts right.
Decide how many places: $10 \to 1$ place, $100 \to 2$ places.
Insert zeros where a place would otherwise be empty.
